channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How television technology is approaching the limits of human vision
item.description: Television displays have evolved dramatically from the bulky cathode ray tube (CRT) sets of the past to today’s ultra-thin and high-resolution screens. Early televisions used electron beams to light u...

item.title: Israeli ambassador declares Iran war will persist until regime collapse despite Trump’s signals of winding down
item.description: Tensions in the ongoing conflict between Israel, the United States, and Iran remain high, with Israel’s ambassador to Washington stating that military operations will continue until the Iranian regime...

item.title: Investigation Launched Into Secret Burial of Bodies in Kericho Mass Grave
item.description: The discovery of 14 bodies secretly buried in a Kericho cemetery has sparked widespread concerns. Authorities were unaware of the burials, which reportedly took place under court-sanctioned orders. Lo...

item.title: Tinubu Talk Say His Government Go Intensify Work to Tackle Insecurity and Ease People Wahala
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don yarn say him government go increase efforts to fight insecurity wey dey affect different parts of Nigeria. Tinubu talk this one for Lagos when him host Vice President Kashim ...

item.title: Short-duration Kilimanjaro climbs gaining momentum among climbers
item.description: Climbers are increasingly opting for short-duration ascents of Mount Kilimanjaro, Africa’s highest peak, with many now attempting to summit in less than 24 hours. The trend, dubbed ultra-marathon clim...

item.title: Gombe Governor Calls for Nationwide Support to Tackle Insecurity
item.description: Gov. Inuwa Yahaya of Gombe State don appeal to Nigerians to show support for the ongoing efforts to fight insecurity across the country. He made this call on Sunday during the Sallah visit from Alhaji...

item.title: DPWH warns public about scammers impersonating officials
item.description: The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) has issued a public advisory warning citizens about scammers pretending to be officials from the agency. These con artists reportedly contact people ...

item.title: Canadian Real Estate Developers Offer Incentives to Boost Home Sales Amid Slump
item.description: With new home sales declining across Canada, real estate developers are stepping up their efforts to attract buyers by offering various incentives. Companies like Mattamy Homes have introduced program...

item.title: UK Government Prepares for Economic Impact of Iran Conflict, Starmer to Lead Response
item.description: Following escalating tensions and recent attacks in the Gulf, UK opposition leader Sir Keir Starmer is set to chair a COBRA meeting on Monday to coordinate the government's response to the potential e...

item.title: Liema Pantsi Wins Big Brother Mzansi Season Six After Successful Comeback
item.description: Liema Pantsi has been crowned the winner of Big Brother Mzansi season six in a memorable redemption story. The 24-year-old from Qonce, Eastern Cape, returned to the house after leaving season four ear...

item.title: Why we sold our six-bedroom Somerset house with orchard and moved back to a two-bedroom London flat
item.description: In May 2020, during lockdown, Pip Rich and his fiancé decided to leave London in search of more space and peace. They sold a one-bedroom central London flat and a four-bedroom suburban house to purcha...

item.title: A Monthly Newspaper Dedicated Exclusively to Positive News
item.description: In today’s fast-paced media environment, most news apps bombard readers with negative headlines, creating stress and anxiety. To counter this, the publication Good Good Good launched the Goodnewspaper...

item.title: Tuggeranong Zone Bowling closure sparks dispute between new landlord and operator over lease and equipment removal
item.description: A popular bowling alley in Tuggeranong, Canberra, is set to close on May 3, 2026, after 30 years of operation, following a failure to agree on lease terms with the building's new owner. The Entertainm...

item.title: Human rights lawyer Marshal Abubakar denies Tracy Ohiri’s allegations, plans legal action to defend his name
item.description: A Nigerian human rights lawyer, Marshal Abubakar, don talk say e go take legal step against one businesswoman, Tracy Ohiri, because she dey make false talk about am. Tracy Ohiri, wey be entrepreneur, ...

item.title: West Bengal to Receive First Supplementary Voter List on March 23 as Part of Electoral Process
item.description: The Election Commission (EC) is set to release the first supplementary voter list for West Bengal on March 23 as part of the ongoing Special Identification Register (SIR) exercise. This list will be i...

item.title: Manchester City claim Carabao Cup after O'Reilly’s quickfire double sinks Arsenal
item.description: Manchester City secured the Carabao Cup with a 2-0 victory over Arsenal at Wembley, thanks to two quick goals from full-back Nico O’Reilly early in the second half. Despite Arsenal sitting nine points...

item.title: Moi University struggles with Sh1 billion debt from botched 1990s construction contract
item.description: Moi University is facing serious financial trouble as it tries to raise over Sh1 billion to settle interest on a Sh185 million debt from a failed construction deal with Vishva Builders Ltd for the Fac...

item.title: Former Ohio teacher pleads guilty to sexual offences with 15-year-old pupil
item.description: A former teacher from Columbus, Ohio, has pleaded guilty to engaging in a sexual relationship with a 15-year-old student in her care. Jamelah Daboubi, aged 28, who taught tenth-grade classes at Horizo...

item.title: India Addresses West Asia Crisis: Modi's Cabinet Focuses on Food, Fuel, and Fertilizer Security
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a crucial cabinet meeting in New Delhi to address the escalating situation in West Asia and its potential impact on India’s essential supplies. The meeting centere...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns advance to CAF Champions League semifinals despite 2-0 second-leg loss and red card
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns secured their place in the CAF Champions League semifinals with a 3-2 aggregate victory over Stade Malien, despite a challenging return leg in Bamako, Mali on Sunday. Coming into the...
